The cryptographic privacy step is the explicit `wireIndistinguishable` field. The PQ transport residual certificate also carries parser/wire exactness as a separate assumption, so wire-format refinement is not hidden inside the cryptographic premise.

The seed-hardened PQ residual certificate proves the accepted handshake's KEM seed implementation boundary is OS-RNG-source-bound and transcript-KDF-bound before it is used by the privacy/ciphertext boundary. The materialized-sidecar PQ certificate additionally puts that seed-hardened residual certificate, accepted materialized sidecar publication facts, sidecar availability/size and wire-replay DA row bindings, tx-leaf ciphertext publication, statement/proof ciphertext vectors, and all-active open-assumption decrypt-to-DA observer facts on one proof object. It does not prove ML-KEM IND-CCA security, AEAD security, HKDF security, OS RNG quality, or full raw-wire implementation equivalence.

The wallet and network regressions prove production encryption is not accidentally deterministic for same plaintext and same recipient under distinct RNG streams and that re-encryption preserves the Lean-modeled public summary shape while changing raw chain and DA wire bytes. They are not proofs of ML-KEM IND-CCA security, AEAD confidentiality, OS RNG quality, simulator-based zero knowledge, or remote topology/timing privacy.

The public metadata privacy certificate keeps raw-wire indistinguishability, proof-system zero knowledge, wallet metadata hygiene, timing/batching policy, and network metadata policy explicit while proving the deterministic public-leakage projection is stable under private/local metadata resampling.

claims.component: network and PQ Noise frame codecs plus bounded network queues
claims.summary: The Lean network resource-admission kernels prove executable frame decode acceptance exactly requires the configured frame-size bound, domain-separated codec marker, successful postcard decode, and full byte consumption, with fail-closed precedence for oversize, missing marker, postcard failure, and trailing bytes. They also prove encode acceptance is exactly the marker-plus-body size bound, every modeled frame marker is four bytes, PQ session plaintext plus AEAD tag equals the ciphertext frame bound, PQ session plaintext is below the ciphertext bound, and network/PQ frame markers are domain-separated. The companion queue-resource admission kernel proves peer-send and event-channel byte-budget reserve/send accounting: messages over cap reject first, counter overflow rejects before aggregate-budget acceptance, aggregate queue over-budget rejects, successful sends increment queued bytes, channel-full/closed send failures roll queued bytes back, and reserve rejection precedes channel state. Generated Lean vectors are checked against the production network HNW codec, pq-noise handshake/session/transcript codecs, and network queue accounting helpers; focused arbitrary-byte mutation corpora additionally compare production network/PQ frame decoders against the marker, bound, postcard success, and full-consumption oracle across malformed, oversized, truncated, trailing-byte, marker-prefixed, and mutated-valid frames. The peer-store capacity kernel separately proves retained peer records are bounded by max_entries, are exactly the recency-prefix subset, and partition with the dropped recency suffix; generated vectors check that production peer-store retention enforces the retained and dropped sets. Focused network regressions additionally require P2P handshake frames to stay under the smaller handshake cap until secure-wire promotion, inbound handshake backlog/timeouts to bound half-open work, address exchange and address books to filter/cap dialable addresses, and peer send loops to avoid awaiting full queues.

Generated vectors bind the modeled frame admission table to production network::wire and pq-noise::codec constants and representative production encode/decode rejection paths without adding runtime work.

Focused arbitrary-byte mutation corpora compare production network::wire and pq-noise::codec frame decoding to an independent marker/bound/postcard/full-consumption oracle over malformed, marker-prefixed, oversized, truncated, trailing-byte, and mutated-valid byte streams; this narrows parser drift risk without claiming a proof of postcard internals.

The Lean queue-resource model covers byte-budget reserve/send accounting for peer-send queues and network event channels, including overflow, over-budget rejection, accepted-send increments, and full/closed rollback; it does not prove tokio mpsc internals, scheduler fairness, peer honesty, or network liveness.

The PQ session ciphertext cap is represented through the plaintext-plus-AEAD-tag theorem and production session constants; this claim does not prove tokio length-delimited transport liveness or full session completion equivalence.

The peer-store capacity theorem covers retention over entries ordered by production recency rank. It proves the retained prefix and dropped suffix partition for that ordered view; peer honesty, address identity quality, and liveness are outside this capacity-retention claim.
